title: Cost Allocation Tag Compliance
spl:
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| eval has_app=isnotnull(resourceTags_user_Application) AND resourceTags_user_Application!=""
| eval has_cc=isnotnull(resourceTags_user_CostCenter) AND resourceTags_user_CostCenter!=""
| stats sum(eval(if(has_app AND has_cc,cost,0))) as tagged_cost sum(cost) as total_cost
| eval tag_compliance_pct=round(100*tagged_cost/total_cost,1)
description: Percentage of monthly spend that is **untagged** or missing required keys (`Application`, `CostCenter`, `Environment`).
value: Percentage of monthly spend that is **untagged** or missing required keys (`Application`, `CostCenter`, `Environment`).
implementation: Expand required keys per policy. Break down by OU/account. Drive tagging enforcement at CI/CD.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: CUR.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:billing:cur`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Expand required keys per policy. Break down by OU/account. Drive tagging enforcement at CI/CD.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| eval has_app=isnotnull(resourceTags_user_Application) AND resourceTags_user_Application!=""
| eval has_cc=isnotnull(resourceTags_user_CostCenter) AND resourceTags_user_CostCenter!=""
| stats sum(eval(if(has_app AND has_cc,cost,0))) as tagged_cost sum(cost) as total_cost
| eval tag_compliance_pct=round(100*tagged_cost/total_cost,1)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cost Allocation Tag Compliance** — Percentage of monthly spend that is **untagged** or missing required keys (`Application`, `CostCenter`, `Environment`).

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:billing:cur`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): CUR. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_billing; **sourcetype**: aws:billing:cur. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_billing, sourcetype="aws:billing:cur", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cost** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_app** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **has_cc** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **tag_compliance_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Single value (tag compliance %), Pie chart (tagged vs untagged $), Table (worst accounts).
visualization: Single value (tag compliance %), Pie chart (tagged vs untagged $), Table (worst accounts).
